description: MarkItDown is a document conversion system that transforms various file formats (PDF, Excel, Word, etc.) into Markdown. The system uses a core engine to orchestrate the conversion process, leveraging a suite of specialized converters and a plugin architecture for extensibility. It supports advanced features like OCR for images and mathematical equation processing for Word documents, and can be exposed as a service via the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

components.name: Core Engine & CLI
components.description: The central orchestrator of the system, responsible for the high-level lifecycle of document conversion. It handles command-line arguments, manages input streams (local files, URLs, or raw data), and implements the plugin discovery mechanism to dynamically load converters.

components.name: CLI Controller
components.description: The entry point for the application, responsible for parsing command-line arguments, managing input/output streams (including local files and URLs), and handling high-level execution flow.

components.name: Conversion Orchestrator
components.description: The core logic that manages the conversion lifecycle. It abstracts input into StreamInfo objects and implements the dispatch mechanism that selects and executes the correct DocumentConverter based on file metadata and MIME types.

components.name: Plugin & Registry Manager
components.description: Responsible for the discovery, registration, and lifecycle management of converters. It manages the internal mapping of file types to converter implementations and facilitates the dynamic loading of external plugins via entry points.

components_relations.relation: Delegates document conversion tasks to the engine after resolving input paths and URLs.
Queries the internal registry to identify and retrieve the appropriate converter strategy for a specific input stream.

components.name: Conversion Suite
components.description: A collection of format-specific converters that implement a unified interface to transform binary or structured data into Markdown. It includes specialized logic for handling complex formats like PDF, Excel, and YouTube transcripts.

components.name: Core Abstractions & Web Converters
components.description: Defines the foundational contracts and result types for all conversions. It also handles web-native or lightweight structured formats like YouTube transcripts and provides the central Markdown formatting logic used across the suite.

components.name: Document & Office Converters
components.description: Manages the conversion of complex, structured document formats including Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), PDF, and HTML. It handles the extraction of text, tables, and structural elements, often using intermediate HTML representations before final Markdown generation.

components.name: Office & Messaging Converters
components.description: Extracts content from Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and Outlook formats. It handles the complexity of XML-based document structures and binary message formats, focusing on preserving semantic hierarchy (headings, lists) and tabular data.

components.name: Web & Semantic Converters
components.description: Processes natively tag-based formats like HTML and EPUB. It serves as both a standalone converter and a critical utility for other components, using beautifulsoup4 and markdownify to transform DOM trees into clean Markdown.

components.name: PDF Extraction Engine
components.description: Manages the high-complexity task of extracting text and structure from PDF files. Unlike tag-based formats, it must reconstruct document semantics (like tables and forms) from raw coordinate-based text and line data.

components.name: Notebook & Specialized Data Converters
components.description: Handles JSON-structured documents, specifically Jupyter Notebooks. It iterates through cell-based structures to interleave code blocks and markdown content into a single stream.

components_relations.relation: Delegates the rendering of complex tables and nested layouts by generating intermediate HTML strings and passing them to the HtmlConverter.
Shares common dependency management patterns for handling optional third-party libraries.
Provides the final Markdown serialization logic for Office components that utilize HTML-based transcoding.
Optionally utilizes HTML processing for notebooks that contain embedded HTML outputs or rich-text cells.
calls

components.name: Intelligent & Multimedia Converters
components.description: Handles non-textual media and advanced document analysis through external AI services and specialized libraries. This includes audio transcription, image captioning, and high-fidelity OCR/layout analysis via Azure Document Intelligence.

components_relations.relation: Implements the standardized DocumentConverterResult interface and utilizes _CustomMarkdownify for consistent HTML-to-Markdown translation.
Wraps transcription and AI analysis outputs into the unified DocumentConverterResult format for consumption by the core engine.
Specialized document converters (like PDF) may delegate complex layout analysis to intelligent services when high-fidelity OCR is required.
calls

components.name: Docx Math Processor
components.description: A specialized internal utility component dedicated to parsing Microsoft Word documents. Its primary responsibility is the complex transformation of Office Math Markup Language (OMML) into LaTeX, ensuring mathematical equations are preserved in a format readable by LLMs.

components.name: Math Integration Orchestrator
components.description: Acts as the entry point for math processing in DOCX files. It scans the document's XML for mathematical tags (e.g., m:oMath), extracts the OMML content, and orchestrates the replacement of these blocks with LaTeX strings generated by the translation engine.

components.name: OMML Translation Engine
components.description: A specialized parser that translates the hierarchical OMML structure into linear LaTeX strings. It utilizes a recursive descent approach with a tag-based dispatch mechanism to handle complex mathematical structures like fractions, radicals, and matrices, while also managing mathematical properties and formatting.

components_relations.relation: Delegates the conversion of identified OMML XML fragments into LaTeX strings during the document pre-processing phase.

components.name: OCR Extension
components.description: An optional enhancement package (markitdown-ocr) that provides Optical Character Recognition capabilities. It wraps standard converters to extract text from images and scanned documents using LLM-based vision services.

components.name: OCR Engine & PDF Handler
components.description: Encapsulates the core OCR logic and the PDF-specific implementation. The LLMVisionOCRService acts as the primary interface for LLM-based text extraction, while PdfConverterWithOCR manages the complex process of rendering or extracting images from PDF pages to be processed by the service.

components.name: Office Document OCR Adapters
components.description: Contains specialized converters for Microsoft Office formats. These adapters extend the base functionality of Word, Excel, and PowerPoint converters by identifying embedded images and shapes, extracting them, and utilizing the central OCR service to convert visual content into markdown-compatible text.

components.name: Plugin Integration Layer
components.description: Serves as the entry point for the markitdown-ocr package. It handles the registration of all OCR-enhanced converters into the main MarkItDown registry, ensuring that the extension's capabilities are seamlessly available to the core engine when the package is installed.

components_relations.relation: Registers the OCR-capable PDF converter and initializes the core OCR service during the plugin loading phase.
Registers the specialized Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) OCR converters into the MarkItDown converter registry.
Invokes the LLMVisionOCRService to perform text extraction on images and visual elements identified within Office documents.

components.name: MCP Server
components.description: The Model Context Protocol implementation that exposes MarkItDown's conversion capabilities as a service. It allows LLMs and external clients to invoke conversion tools over HTTP/SSE via a Starlette-based server.

components.name: Plugin Registry
components.description: The extensibility mechanism and sample implementations that demonstrate how to add new format support to the core engine. It defines the registration protocol used by both internal and third-party extensions.

components.name: Plugin Discovery & Registration Engine
components.description: This component serves as the central coordinator for the extensibility mechanism. It is responsible for the "lazy" discovery of external plugins using importlib.metadata and managing the global state of the converter registry. It defines the ConverterRegistration data structure and the priority protocol, ensuring that converters are correctly ordered and selected during the conversion process.

components.name: Extension Implementations (Plugins)
components.description: This component encompasses the concrete implementations of the plugin protocol, ranging from simple reference examples to complex production-grade suites. These plugins implement the register_converters interface, which the engine invokes to populate the registry. They provide specialized DocumentConverter subclasses for various formats and advanced analysis.

components_relations.relation: Discovers and initializes external plugins by invoking their register_converters entry point during the engine's initialization or when plugins are explicitly enabled.
Registers concrete DocumentConverter instances into the engine's internal registry, specifying the target file formats and their relative priority.
Routes conversion requests to the appropriate format-specific converter.
Discovers and loads external converters during initialization.
The DocxConverter delegates XML math parsing to this component during the pre-processing phase.
Registers specialized OCR-enabled converters into the core registry.
Invokes the core conversion logic to fulfill remote client requests.
Provides the entry points and registration logic for extending the core's capabilities.
